campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review transportation funding reports, adopt a new policy about how city property can be used, and discuss efforts to fill city job openings and keep employees. The meeting included a presentation from the regional transit authority covering their investment activities from March 2026.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority monthly investment report for March 2026
New city policy restricting use of city-owned property for federal civil enforcement
City job vacancies and strategies to recruit and retain employees

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing on plans to clean up hazardous conditions on certain properties identified by the county, and residents had a chance to voice objections. The council also adopted a new technology policy for the city and established a process for residents to request accommodations they may need.
keyTopics: Hazardous property abatement and cleanup orders
New City Technology Disruption Policy
Process for requesting reasonable accommodations

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — May 19, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on May 19, 2026, to approve routine administrative items including meeting minutes and financial reports. The council authorized several agreements and contracts, including a five-year services deal, a construction contract with O'Grady Paving, and accepted a donation of emergency medical equipment (AED).
keyTopics: Approved March 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized O'Grady Paving construction contract
Accepted donation of Automated External Defibrillator (AED)
Approved grant applications for state funding
Adopted project list for Fiscal Year 2026-27

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 20, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to plan upcoming agendas and update committee work plans. The May 26 meeting was cancelled, so the council reviewed draft items for their June 2 meeting and discussed adding new business and scheduling time-sensitive agenda items.
keyTopics: June 2, 2026 draft agenda items and additions
Neighborhood Services and Education Committee Work Plan update
Rules and Open Government Committee Work Plan
Council-sponsored events approval

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 20, 2026
mountain-view.summary: Mountain View's City Council met on May 20, 2026 to approve meeting minutes, hear from residents during public comment, and select new members for the Youth Advisory Committee for the upcoming fiscal year. The council also reviewed various board and committee agendas.
keyTopics: Youth Advisory Committee member selection for fiscal year 2026-27
Public comment period for resident input
Board and commission agenda planning

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 19, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a regular meeting on May 19, 2026, to conduct city business and recognize two important observances. The council honored Asian Pacific American Heritage Month and Affordable Housing Month during the meeting.
keyTopics: Asian Pacific American Heritage Month recognition
Affordable Housing Month observance
April meeting minutes approval

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 19, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met on May 19, 2026 to update the city's long-term health and safety plans, recognize the Public Works Department for their work on roads, utilities, and maintenance, and celebrate Asian American Pacific Islander Month.
keyTopics: Update to Health and Safety Element of General Plan (2015-2040)
Public Works Department recognition during National Public Works Week
Asian American Pacific Islander (AAPI) Month celebration
City services including streets, sidewalks, trees, facilities, and transportation

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's Station Area Task Force held a special meeting on May 21, 2026, to discuss topics related to the station area. The full agenda details were not included in the notice provided.
keyTopics: Station Area Task Force special meeting
Met at City Hall Council Chambers (1500 Warburton Avenue) and via Zoom
Meeting held in compliance with California Brown Act requirements

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 19, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting to address several land development and zoning matters, including new ordinances related to city regulations. The council also appointed a permanent city manager and approved a site development permit amendment with an associated map for a local project.
keyTopics: New city ordinances and zoning regulations being adopted
Site development permit amendment and property map approval
Permanent city manager appointment
Assessment confirmation and ordering (likely for infrastructure or services)

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 15, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council discussed plans to help Palo Alto residents switch from gas to electric heating and appliances in their homes. The council reviewed proposed principles for electrification programs designed to help the city comply with air quality regulations from the regional Air District.
keyTopics: Residential electrification status update and progress
Proposed principles for home electrification assistance programs
Air District regulation compliance requirements
Staff presentation on electrification program details
